41.reason: [The loop condition checks `op != EOF`, but `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not the value of `op`. When input ends, `scanf` returns EOF (typically -1), which gets stored in `op`, but the loop condition will then compare that -1 against EOF again incorrectly. Also, if `scanf` fails to read an integer (e.g., at end-of-file), `op` remains uninitialized or holds previous value, leading to incorrect behavior. The correct approach is to check the return value of `scanf` directly in the loop condition.]
